title: SSL/TLS-enforcement contract (authored for the SSL/TLS-enforcement testing task)
description: The target must refuse plain HTTP (redirect to HTTPS, no API data), refuse TLS 1.0/1.1, accept TLS 1.2/1.3 and serve the endpoint, present a non-expired CA-signed cert whose CN/SAN matches the host (chain of trust ok, not self-signed), and offer no weak cipher suite (RC4/DES/3DES/EXPORT/NULL). Agents construct the TLS test plan from this contract; ground truth is the live fixture's observed behavior. The DummyJSON app is NEVER modified: a local, air-gapped TLS terminator (tls_fixture.py) stands in front of the untouched DummyJSON and is the system under test for TLS enforcement. All probing is handshake + read-only GET only.

target.note: tls_fixture.py terminates TLS (1.2/1.3 only, strong ciphers, CA-signed leaf) and forwards read-only GETs to the unmodified DummyJSON upstream; its plaintext listener 301-redirects to HTTPS.
